title: Mighty Hunter
description: You are a hunter of the Great Wood, the best the town has seen in generations. You know every part of the Wood within a two-day march.

outcomes.text: You start with both the Expert Tracker move and the Stalker move. Go mark them now.

title: Wide Wanderer
description: You have travelled much of the known world and perhaps parts beyond. Add each of the following to the Neighbors list in the Stonetop playbook, choosing 1 trait for each:

bullets: Ennis (from Marshedge)
Shahar (from Gordon's Delve)
Yannic (from the Hillfolk)
Tovia (from Lygos)
Sasca (from the northern Manmarch)

outcomes.text: You start with the Mental Map move. Mark it now. When you Know Things about the wider world, you can roll +WIS instead of +INT.
When you arrive somewhere you've visited before (your call), tell the GM when you were last here, and the GM will tell you how it's changed.

title: Beast-Bonded (+Wisdom)
description: You grew up civilized, but your soul is bound to a beast of the wild. You're closer to it than to any man or woman. How did this bond come about? How long ago?

outcomes.text: You start with the Animal Companion move. Go mark it now.

When you focus on your animal companion for a few moments, you can use any of the actions you've marked below, no matter the distance between you. Mark 1 action at 1st level, then another at 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th.

pick: Gauge its distance and direction from you
Call it back to your side
Get a brief impression of what it senses
Lend it your strength—lose 1d6 HP, and it regains an equal amount

title: Special Possessions (pick 2)
pick: ◇ Compound bow (far, +1 damage, x piercing; low ammo, all out)
Distillery: skins of fine whisky (2 uses, grants advantage to Persuade), copper tubes, malt, ◇◇ firkins, stills, barrels, etc.
Hideouts: expend a use to have a well-stocked, safe shelter nearby; GM can veto.
Husbandry tools: brushes, muzzles, collars, feed, whips, bridles, etc. Gain advantage to Persuade domestic beasts (livestock, dogs, etc.).
Hounds: 2-3 followers, tick the move below
Lay of the land: expend a use to know where to find ___, without having to Know Things; GM can veto.
Trapping gear: snares, pelts, musk, bait, etc.When you Forage, get +1 use of provisions

title: Hounds
description: 2-3 followers (trackers, keen-nosed, fast); HP 6; Damage d6 (hand, grabby); Instinct: to give chase; Cost: training.

title: Alpha (Requires level 6+, and Wild Speech or Spirit Tongue)
description: When you assert dominance over another (beast, spirit, Fae, or person), roll +Wisdom:

outcomes.text: They must pick 1, and you have advantage on your next roll against them:
bullets: Accept your authority, at least for now
Slink away or flee, then avoid you
Fight you for dominance

title: Animal Companion
description: You are accompanied by a beast of uncommon loyalty and cleverness.

title: Big Game Hunter
description: When you strike at the weak spot of a large or huge creature, you deal +2 damage.

title: Blot Out the Sun
description: When you Let Fly with a bow, you can deplete your ammunition (mark the next ammo status after your weapon) before you roll. If you do, choose 1:

bullets: Gain advantage on your damage roll
Add the area tag to your attack; roll damage separately for each target

title: Call the Shot (+Dexterity)
description: When you take your time and calmly line up the perfect shot, either deal your damage or roll +Dexterity

bullets: Ignore armor or deal +1d4 damage (your call)
Stun, hobble, or hinder them
Make them trip or drop what they're holding
Do no harm; don't deal your damage after all

title: Expert Tracker (+Wisdom)
description: When you Seek Insight by searching for or studying the signs left by passing creatures, you can ask "What happened here recently?" for free, even on a 6-. When you follow a creature's trail, roll +Wisdom:

outcomes.text: You follow it to a significant change in terrain or activity, and you can ask the GM a reasonable question about your quarry and get a useful answer.
You follow it to a significant change in terrain or activity.

title: Home on the Range
description: When a journey requires you to Defy Danger or Struggle as One, treat a 6- as a 7-9.

title: Magnificent Specimen (Requires Animal Companion)
description: Each time you take this move, your companion gains 2 additional options of your choice.

title: Mental Map
description: You can always retrace your steps and can accurately gauge distances and directions. You might not know the way forward but can always find your way back. When you think back on a place you've been, you can Seek Insight retroactively, as if you were still there.

title: Naturalist
description: When you Know Things about beasts, natural environs, or spirits of the wild, you have advantage.

title: On the Hoof
description: When you travel through the wilderness, you can procure 1d6 uses of provisions each day (roll with disadvantage in winter or barren terrain). Provisions can substitute for supplies when you Make Camp.

title: Pack Horse
description: You can carry up to 4 ◇ with a light load, 7 ◇ with a normal load, and 10 ◇ with a heavy load.

title: Predator
description: When you Seek Insight, add the following to the list of questions you can ask:

bullets: Who or what here is the easiest prey?
How is ________ weak or vulnerable?

outcomes.text: When acting on the answer to either question, deal an extra 1d4 damage.

title: A Safe Place
description: When you select and prepare the party's camp site, hold 1 Precaution, or 2 Precaution if you are well versed with this area and its dangers

outcomes.text: If trouble finds your camp site, you can spend 1 Precaution to reveal a simple defense, warning, or trick that you prepared in advance. If you do, tell us how you knew to make that specific preparation.

title: Sniff Out Corruption
description: When you Seek Insight, you can ask, "What here stinks of the unnatural?" for free, even on a 6-.

title: Stalker
description: When you carry a normal or light load and move with care, you make no noise and leave no sign of your passing. When you hide yourself in a natural environment, you remain unseen until you draw attention to yourself, move positions, or attack.

title: Wild Speech
description: The grunts, barks, chirps, and calls of natural beasts are as a language to you. You can understand their intentions and communicate basic ideas. When you Persuade a beast, you can choose to roll +Wisdom.

title: Worldly (Requires level 2+ and the Ranger)
description: Take a move from the Blessed, Fox, Heavy, Marshal, or Seeker playbooks, for which you otherwise qualify. You can pick from a different playbook each time. You can't pick Improved Stat or Superior Stat.

title: Beast of Legend (Requires level 6+ and Magnificent Specimen)
description: Each time you take this move, pick 1:

bullets: They are exceptional (and roll +2 instead of +1)
They get +4 HP and +1 armor
They develop some unique ability or trait

title: Constant Vigilance (Requires level 6+)
description: Unless you're dazed, you're never caught off guard—not even when asleep or if you roll a 6-. When you intercept a sudden threat (to yourself or an ally), you have advantage on whatever move you make.

title: Giant Slayer (Requires level 6+ and Big Game Hunter)
description: When you strike at a weak spot of a large or huge creature, you deal another +2 damage (+4 total).

title: Master Tracker (Requires level 6+ and Expert Tracker)
description: When you even briefly scan for tracks or other signs left by passing creatures, you can ask the GM, 'What happened here recently?' and get an honest answer—no need to Seek Insight.

title: Trailblazer (Requires level 6+ and Home on the Range)
description: When a journey causes you to Defy Danger or Struggle as One, on a 10+ you also learn or discover something interesting and useful—ask the GM what.

title: Walk It Off (Requires level 6+)
description: When you would mark a debility, you can mark this move instead to no ill effect. Clear it as you would a debility.

title: Warden of the Wild
description: When you defeat a perversion of nature, you can ask the GM 2 of the following and get a useful answer:

bullets: Will it come back? If so, how can I stop it?
Will its taint spread? If so, how can I contain it?
What useful (but grisly) bits can I harvest?
What else can I learn about it or its ilk?

title: Survivalist
description: When you Forage, pick 1 extra choice (even on a 6-, pick 1) and add "Find or fashion some useful item or supply (GM can veto)" to the list of options.

title: Pathfinder
description: When you lead your people to Pull Together or Deploy beyond sight of home, you have advantage.

title: Instinct (Choose 1)
description: You can change on levelling or when appropriate story events affect you.
pickOne: ADVENTURE - To test yourself, to experience new things.
INDEPENDENCE - To refuse help and push others away.
STEWARDSHIP - To value beasts and natural places over people.
TENACITY - To be stubborn, to persist.
WONDER - To marvel at beauty, magnificence, splendor.
